questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.solution:
$f(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{n^2}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{n^2+2}}+\ldots .+\frac{1}{\sqrt{n^2+2 n+1}}$
Terms of the sequence are decreasing and number of terms are $(2 n+2)$
$\frac{2 n+2}{\sqrt{n^2+Z n+1}} \leq f(x) \leq \frac{2 n+2}{\sqrt{n^2}}$
Now $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{2(n+1)}{\sqrt{n^2+2 n+1}}=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{2 n\left(1+\frac{1}{n}\right)}{n \sqrt{1+\frac{2}{n}+\frac{1}{n^2}}}$
Similarly $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{2(n+1)}{\sqrt{n^2}}=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{2(n+1)}{n}=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
If $f(x)=\frac{\log \left(x^2+e^x\right)}{\log \left(x^4+e^{2 x}\right)}$ and $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} f(x)=\ell$ and $\lim _{x \rightarrow-\infty} f(x)=m$ then find the value of $\left[\frac{2 \ell}{m}\right]$ is $\qquad$
(Where [.] denote the greatest integer function)
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{\log \left(x^2+e^x\right)}{\log \left(x^4+e^{2 x}\right)}=\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{\log e^x\left(1+\frac{x^2}{e^x}\right)}{\log e^{2 x}\left(1+\frac{x^4}{e^{2 x}}\right)} \\ & \Rightarrow \lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{x+\log \left(1+\frac{x^2}{e^x}\right)}{2 x+\log \left(1+\frac{x^4}{e^{2 x}}\right)}=\frac{1+\frac{1}{x} \log \left(1+\frac{x^2}{e^x}\right)}{2+\frac{1}{x} \log \left(1+\frac{x^4}{e^{2 x}}\right)} \Rightarrow \operatorname{as}_{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{x^2}{e^x} \rightarrow 0 \Rightarrow \operatorname{as}_{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{x^2}{e^{2 x}} \rightarrow 0 \\ & \therefore \ell=\frac{1}{2} \cdot m=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow\left[\frac{2 \ell}{m}\right]=\left[\frac{2 \times \frac{1}{2}}{\frac{1}{2}}\right]=2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\sum_{r=1}^n \cos \frac{r \pi}{2 n}\right)\left(\sum_{r=1}^n \cos ^2 \frac{r \pi}{2 n}\right)\left(\sum_{r=1}^n \cos ^3 \frac{r \pi}{2 n}\right)\left(\sum_{r=1}^n \cos ^4 \frac{r \pi}{2 n}\right)\left(e^{\frac{1}{n}}-1\right)^4=\frac{k}{\pi^2}$ then find the value of $\frac{1}{k}$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \int_0^1 \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2} x\right) d x \cdot \int_0^1 \cos ^2\left(\frac{\pi}{2} x\right) d x \cdot \int_0^1 \cos ^3\left(\frac{\pi}{2} x\right) d x \cdot \int_0^1 \cos ^4\left(\frac{\pi}{2} x\right) d x \\ & =\left(\frac{2}{\pi}\right)^4\left(\int_0^{\pi / 2} \cos ^2 t d t\right)\left(\int_0^{\pi / 2} \cos ^3 t d t\right)\left(\int_0^{\pi / 2} \cos ^4 t d t\right)=\left(\frac{2}{\pi}\right)^4 1 \cdot \frac{\pi}{4} \cdot \frac{2}{3} \cdot \frac{3 \pi}{16}=\frac{1}{2 \pi^2}=\frac{k}{\pi^2} \\ & \therefore \frac{1}{k}=2 \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Find the minimum value of the expression $f(x)=A x^2+B x+C$ whose zeroes are $\beta$ and $\beta+2$ which can be obtained by the relation $\beta=[2 \alpha-1]$ (where 1] denote the greatest integer function) and $\alpha=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{1-\cos (1-\cos x)}{x^4}$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \alpha=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{1-\left\{1-\frac{(1-\cos x)^2}{2!}+\frac{(1-\cos x)^4}{4!}+\ldots . .\right\}}{x^4} \Rightarrow \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\frac{(1-\cos x)^2}{2!}\left\{1-\frac{(1-\cos x)^2}{2 \cdot 3!}+\ldots . .\right\}}{x^4} \\
& \Rightarrow \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\left(2 \sin ^2 \frac{x}{2}\right)^2}{2 x^4}\left[1-\frac{(1-\cos x)^2}{2 \cdot 3!} \ldots . .\right] \Rightarrow \lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left[1-\frac{(1-\cos x)^2}{2 \cdot 3!}+\ldots . .\right] \Rightarrow \alpha=\frac{1}{8} \\
& \beta=[2 \alpha-1]=\left[2 \times \frac{1}{8}-1\right]=\left[-\frac{3}{4}\right]=-1
\end{aligned}$

Now, $\beta=-1, \beta+2=-1+2=1$
The quadratic expression is $x^2-(-1+1) x+(-1 \times(-1 \times 1)$
$f(x)=x^2-1 \Rightarrow f(x)=2 x-0=0 \Rightarrow x=0$

Minimum value is $(-1)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $\operatorname{Lim}_{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{1}{2 n+1}+\frac{1}{2 n+3}+\frac{1}{2 n+5}+\ldots \ldots .+\frac{1}{4 n-1}\right)=\frac{A}{B} \ln C$, where $A, B, C \in N$. Find the least value of $A+B+C$.
questions.solution:
$L=\operatorname{Lim}_{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{1}{2 n+1}+\frac{1}{2 n+2}+\frac{1}{2 n+3}+\ldots \ldots .+\frac{1}{4 n}\right)-\operatorname{Lim}_{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{1}{2 n+2}+\frac{1}{2 n+4}+\frac{1}{2 n+6}+\ldots \ldots . .+\frac{1}{4 n}\right)$
$=\operatorname{Lim}_{n \rightarrow \infty}\left[\frac{1}{n} \sum_{r=1}^{2 n} \frac{n}{2 n+r}-\frac{1}{n} \sum_{r=1}^n \frac{n}{2 n+2 r}\right]=\operatorname{Lim}_{n \rightarrow \infty}\left[\frac{1}{n} \sum_{r=1}^{2 n} \frac{1}{2+\frac{r}{n}}-\frac{1}{n} \sum_{r=1}^n \frac{1}{2+2\left(\frac{r}{n}\right)}\right]$
$=\int_0^2 \frac{1}{2+x} d x-\int_0^1 \frac{1}{2+2 x} d x=[\ln (2+x)]_0^2-\frac{1}{2}[\ln (1+x)]_0^1$
$=\ln 4-\ln 2-\frac{1}{2} \ln 2=\left(2-\frac{3}{2}\right) \ln 2=\frac{1}{2} \ln 2=\frac{\mathrm{A}}{\mathrm{B}} \ln \mathrm{C}$
Hence least value of $A+B+C=1+2+2=5$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $f(x)$ and $g(x)$ be continuous on $R$ (set of real numbers).
If $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(x)}{\sin ^2 x}=8, \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{g(x)}{2 \cos x-x e^x+x^3+x-2}=\lambda$ and $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}(1+2 f(x))^{1 / g(x)}=\frac{1}{e}$, then find the value of $\lambda$.
questions.solution:
$$
\because \quad \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(x)}{\sin ^2 x}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\frac{f(x)}{x^2}}{\frac{\sin ^2 x}{x^2}}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(x)}{x^2}=8 \text { and } \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} f(x)=0
$$
and $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{g(x)}{2\left(1-\frac{x^2}{2 !}+\ldots \ldots\right)-x\left(1+x+\frac{x^2}{2 !}+\ldots \ldots\right)+x^3+x-2}=\lambda$
$\begin{gathered}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{g(x)}{x^2\left(-2+\frac{x}{2}+\ldots \ldots\right)}=1 \Rightarrow \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{g(x)}{-2 x^2}=\lambda \\ \therefore \quad \lim _{x \rightarrow 0}(1+2 f(x))^{1 / g(x)}=e^{\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2 f(x) / x^2}{g(x) / x^2}}=e^{-8 / \lambda}=\frac{1}{e} \Rightarrow \lambda=8\end{gathered}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: Let $\lim _{x \rightarrow \frac{\pi}{2}}\left(2 x \tan x-\frac{\pi}{\cos x}\right)=\lambda$, then find the value of $|\lambda|$.
questions.solution:
Let $x=\frac{\pi}{2}-h$
$\therefore \lim _{h \rightarrow 0}\left((\pi-2 h) \cot h-\frac{\pi}{\sin h}\right)=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{\pi \cos h}{\sin h}-\frac{\pi}{\sin h}-\frac{2 h}{\tan h}\right)=-2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
If $\lim _{t \rightarrow x} \frac{\int_x^t \sin ^{-1}(n z) d z}{t^2-x^2}=f_n(x)$ then find the value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\left[f_2(x)\right]+\left[f_4(x)\right]\right)$
[Note: $[k]$ denotes greatest integer function less than or equal to $k$ ]
questions.solution:
$$
f_n(x)=\lim _{t \rightarrow x} \frac{\sin ^{-1} n t}{2 t}=\frac{\sin ^{-1} n x}{2 x}
$$

Now, $\quad \lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\left[\frac{\sin ^2 2 x}{2 x}\right]+\left[\frac{\sin ^{-1} 4 x}{2 x}\right]\right)=1+2=3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)$ be a derivable function satisfying $f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y) \forall x, y \in R$ and $f^{\prime}(0)=1$. If $A=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2^{f(\tan x)}-2^{f(\sin x)}}{x^3}$, then find the value of $e^{4 A}$.
questions.solution:
Differentiating w.r.t. $y$ keeping $x$ constant, we get
$$
f^{\prime}(x+y) \cdot 1=f^{\prime}(y)
$$
Putting $y=0, f^{\prime}(x)=1 \Rightarrow f(x)=x+C$
Putting $x=y=0$ in given relation, $f(0)=0$
$\therefore \quad C=0$
$\therefore \quad f(x)=x$
$\begin{aligned} A & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2^{\tan x}-2^{\sin x}}{x^3}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2^{\sin x}\left(2^{\tan x-\sin x}-1\right)}{(\tan x-\sin x)} \times\left(\frac{\tan x-\sin x}{x^3}\right) \\ & =1 \times \ln 2 \times \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\tan x(1-\cos x)}{x^3}=\frac{\ln 2}{2}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{array}{lll}\therefore & 4 A & =2 \ln 2=\ln 4 \\ \therefore & e^{4 A} & =4\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: If the value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{1}{x^4}-\frac{\int_0^{x^2} e^{-y^2} d y}{x^6}\right)=\frac{a}{b}$ (where $a$ and $b$ are co-prime) then find the value of $(a+b)$.

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: If the value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\ln \left(1+\sin ^3 x \cos ^2 x\right) \cot \left(\ln ^3(1+x)\right) \tan ^4 x}{\sin \left(\sqrt{x^2+2}-\sqrt{2}\right) \cdot \ln \left(1+x^2\right)}=\sqrt{n}$ where $n \in N$, then find the value of $n$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\ln \left(1+\sin ^3 x \cos ^2 x\right) \cot \left(\ln ^3(1+x)\right) \tan ^4 x}{\sin \left(\sqrt{x^2+2}-\sqrt{2}\right) \cdot \ln \left(1+x^2\right)}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\left(\sin ^3 x \cos ^2 x\right) x}{\left(\sqrt{x^2+2}-\sqrt{2}\right) x^2} \\ & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2 \sqrt{2} \sin ^2 x}{x^2}=2 \sqrt{2}=\sqrt{8} \Rightarrow n=8\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(n)=\ln \left(n^2-1\right)-\ln \left(n^2+2 n\right), n \in N, n \geq 2$. If $L=\lim _{m \rightarrow \infty}\left(e^{\sum^{e^m / 2} /(n)}-1\right) m^\alpha$ exists and has non-zero finite value, then find the value of $(\alpha+L)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \sum_{n=2}^m f(n) & =\ln \left(\frac{3}{m^2+2 m}\right) \\ \therefore \quad e^{\sum_{n=2}^m f(n)} & =\frac{3}{m^2+2 m} \\ L & =\lim _{m \rightarrow \infty}\left(e^{\frac{3}{m^2+2 m}}-1\right) m^\alpha=\lim _{m \rightarrow \infty} \frac{3 m^\alpha}{m^2+2 m} \Rightarrow \alpha=2 \text { and } L=3 \\ \therefore \quad \alpha+L & =5\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $f$ be a real valued derivable function such that $f(x) f(y)=f(x) y+x f(y), \forall x, y \in R$. If $f^{\prime}(0)=2$ then find $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left[\frac{f(x)}{\sin x}\right]$.
[Note: [] represents greatest integer function.]
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \because \quad \frac{f(x) f(y)}{x y}=\frac{f(x)}{x}+\frac{f(y)}{y} \\ & \text { Let } \quad F(x)=\frac{f(x)}{x} \\ & \end{aligned}$
$\begin{array}{rlrl} & \therefore \quad F(0) & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(x)}{x}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{f^{\prime}(x)}{1}=2 \\ & \therefore \quad F(x) F(y) & =F(x)+F(y) \\ \text { Putting } \quad y & =0,2 F(x)=F(x)+2 \Rightarrow F(x)=2 \\ & \therefore & f(x) & =2 x \\ & \therefore & \lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left[\frac{2 x}{\sin x}\right] & =2\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $L=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n^3} \sum_{k=1}^n k^2 e^{\frac{k}{n}}$, then find the value of $e-L$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} L & =\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n^3} \sum_{k=1}^n k^2 e^{k / n}=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n} \sum_{k=1}^n\left(\frac{k}{n}\right)^2 e^{k / n}=\int_0^1 x^2 e^x d x \\ L & =\left.\left(x^2-2 x+2\right) e^x\right|_0 ^1=e-2 \\ \Rightarrow e-L & =2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Two cubic function $f(x)=x^3+a x^2+b x+c$ and $g(x)=c x^3+b x^2+a x+1$ satisfy the following.
(i) $f(3)=0, g(4)=0$
(ii) The value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow p} \frac{f(x)}{g(x)}$ exists for all $p \in R-\{4\}$
Find the value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow-1} \frac{f(x)+g(x)}{x+1}$.
questions.solution:
$$
f(x)=x^3+a x^2+b x+c=0
$$

Put $x=1 / t$, we get
$$
c t^3+b t^2+a t+1=0=g(t)
$$

Hence, roots of $f(x)$ and $g(x)$ are reciprocal to each other.
Also, $g(1 / 3)=0$ hence for $\lim _{x \rightarrow p} \frac{f(x)}{g(x)}$ exists for all $p \in R-\{4\}$ $f(1 / 3)$ is also 0 .
$\therefore$ Roots of $f(x)$ are $x^3+a x^2+b x+c=0 < { }_{1 / 4}^3$
Now,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \lim _{x \rightarrow-1} \frac{f(x)+g(x)}{x+1}=3(c+1)-(a+b) \\
& x^3+a x^2+b x+c=0 < \begin{array}{l}
1 / 4 \\
1 / 3
\end{array}
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
-a & =3+\frac{1}{4}+\frac{1}{3}=\frac{43}{12} \Rightarrow a=\frac{-43}{12} \\
b & =\frac{3}{4}+\frac{1}{12}+1=\frac{11}{6}=\frac{22}{12} \\
c & =\frac{-1}{4}
\end{aligned}
$$

Now, $\lim _{x \rightarrow-1} \frac{f(x)+g(x)}{x+1}=3(c+1)-(a+b)=3\left(\frac{-1}{4}+1\right)-\left(\frac{-43}{12}+\frac{22}{12}\right)=\frac{9}{4}+\frac{7}{4}=4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{1}{x^5}\left(\int_0^x e^{-t^2} d t\right)-\frac{1}{x^4}+\frac{1}{3 x^2}\right)$ is equal to $\frac{m}{n}(m, n$ are co-prime), find $(m+n)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{3} \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{3 \int_0^x e^{-t^2} d t-3 x+x^3}{x^5}\left(\frac{\infty}{0}\right) \\ & =\frac{1}{3} \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{3 e^{-x^2}-3+3 x^2}{5 x^4}=\frac{1}{5} \lim _{t \rightarrow 0} \frac{e^{-t}-1+t}{t^2}=\frac{1}{10}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

Let

$$
then find the value of $\ln A$ at $n=11$.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{n} \sqrt{n+1}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{n} \sqrt{n+2}}+\ldots \ldots+\frac{1}{\sqrt{n} \sqrt{n+n}}\right)$ is equal to $a \sqrt{b}-\frac{c}{d}$, where $a, b, c$ and $d$ are positive integer, $c$ and $d$ are co-prime. Find the value of $\left(a^4+b^3+c^2+d\right)$.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
L & =\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{n} \sqrt{n+1}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{n} \sqrt{n+2}}+\ldots+\frac{1}{\sqrt{n} \sqrt{n+n}}\right) \\
& =\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{r=1}^n \frac{1}{\sqrt{n} \sqrt{n+r}}=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{r=1}^n\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{n\left(1+\frac{r}{n}\right)}}\right)=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{r=1}^n\left(\frac{1}{n} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{1+\frac{r}{n}}}\right) \\
& =\int_0^1 \frac{1}{\sqrt{1+x}} d x=2 \sqrt{2}-2
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence $a=2, b=2, c=2, d=1$
$$
\therefore a^4+b^3+c^2+d=29
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
